_description: Thin project settings, scaffolded by /init-project → copied to .claude/settings.json. Framework behavior (agents, skills, commands, hooks, MCP) lives in the `cr_adlc_framework` plugin; this file holds only what a plugin cannot own: local Bash/permission policy, OTEL env, and plugin enablement.
_COMMENT_MCP: The MCP servers live in the project-root .mcp.json written by /init-project with THIS project's host-port block (ADR-0035 — the plugin ships no .mcp.json since 0.46.0). Auto-approve them so the session connects without a trust prompt.

env._COMMENT_OTEL: OpenTelemetry exporter for cost + token audit, pointed at the local collector from infra/adlc/. Set project=<your-slug> below to separate this project's telemetry.
env._COMMENT_CENTRAL: Central observability egress is OPT-IN (ADR-0004) and configured in infra/adlc/.env (gitignored) — the endpoint + API key live there, never in this committed file. The identity below is non-secret; AGENTIC_DEVELOPER_HASH is a salted hash, never an email.

permissions._COMMENT_DENY: The network/publish egress deny entries (docker push/login, curl, wget, ssh, scp, rsync) and the host-side package-install deny were retired with the local-only boundary (see the local-only-retirement ADR); the only remaining defense against agent network egress is post-hoc trace audit. The PreToolUse hook still enforces write-path scoping, the test-immutability firewall, and the narrow Bash safety guards (host-side runserver, unscoped `docker compose down`). Removing these denies is a deliberate, human-approved deny-list weakening surfaced at the gate; the 'never weaken the deny-list silently' rule now guards the remaining write-path/safety denials.
